category|Category:Umajiisobe-jinja}} [[JA:馬路石邊神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11668076}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Umajiisobe Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 本殿.JPG|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|03|37.3|N|135|59|58.8|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Umajiisobe Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = (trad.) {{jalink|Hakuhō|Q11580612}} 3 ({{jalink|663|Q31122}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}}{{jalink|hiwadabuki|Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|May 4|Q2549}} |神事 = Hōnen-odori (May 4) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Umaji
Isobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Umajiisobe Shrine''' (うまじいそべじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} its rank was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. Its shrine crest is the "mokkō". === Enshrined deities === The principal deities are the following two: * '''{{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}''' === History === It is said to have been founded in {{jalink|Hakuhō|Q11580612}} 3 ({{jalink|663|Q31122}}), during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}}, and that in {{jalink|Shuchō|Q777413}} 1 ({{jalink|686|Q30619}}) Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto was {{jalink|jointly enshrined|Q11412524}}. In ancient times, it was worshipped as the {{jalink|ujigami|Q767655}} by the {{jalink|Isobe no Kimi clan||Isobe-no-Kimi}} family, a {{jalink|local powerful clan|Q84066}} based in {{jalink|Umaji-gō}}. In the mid-{{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} lists it as "Umajiisobe Shrine, Yasu District, {{jalink|Ōmi Province|Q843882}}," placing it among shikinaisha. In the medieval period, as the {{jalink|guardian shrine|Q13479654}} of {{jalink|Tanaka-no-shō}}, it was called "Tanaka Daimyōjin" and "Tanaka Tennō-sha." In the Sengoku period, it was revered by the {{jalink|Rokkaku clan|Q2975342}}, but shrine buildings were lost in the conflicts of {{jalink|Genki|Q1145064}} and {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}}. It was rebuilt in the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, and the current buildings date to {{jalink|Bunkyū|Q730266}} 2 ({{jalink|1862|Q7698}}). In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 9 ({{jalink|1876|Q7766}}), it was ranked village shrine in the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, and in Meiji 14 it was promoted to {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Precincts === ファイル:馬路石邊神社 拝殿.JPG|'''Haiden''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 神門.JPG|'''Shinmon gate''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 鳥居.JPG|'''Torii''' The current shrine buildings were constructed in {{jalink|Bunkyū|Q730266}} 2 ({{jalink|1862|Q7698}}). Along the approach stands a "Mikoshi-zuka" mound said to contain a buried mikoshi. === Subsidiary shrines === [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 摂末社.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Subsidiary shrines]] The subsidiary shrines are grouped on the east side of the precincts. * Kotoshironushi Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - deities: {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} * Shinmei Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu Kōtaimikami}} * Hachiman Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997|Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} * Sannō Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * Inari Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} * Atago Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Homusubi-no-Kami}} === Main festivals === * Spring festival (May 4-5){{Cite web|title="Nakasendō Moriyama-juku" complete guidebook|url=https://www.city.moriyama.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/004/384/gai.pdf |page=4|website=Moriyama and Yoshimi district revitalization committee |accessdate=2025-03-18 |language=ja}} *: On May 5, the Hōnen-odori (Moriyama City designated intangible folk cultural property) is offered. * Autumn festival (third Saturday in October) *: Sōmoku Sentō (kyōgen offering) is performed. === Local information === ; Location * {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 ; Access * Nearest station: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125|JR West}}{{jalink|Biwako Line|Q804135}} {{jalink|Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|Moriyama Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=441 Umajiisobe Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うましいそへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Umajiisobe-jinja}} [[JA:馬路石邊神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11668076}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Umajiisobe Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 本殿.JPG|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|03|37.3|N|135|59|58.8|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Umajiisobe Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549}} |神事 = Hōnen-odori (May 4) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Umaji
Isobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Umajiisobe Shrine''' (うまじいそべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Its shrine crest is the "mokkō". === Enshrined deities === The principal deities are the following two: * '''{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}''' === History === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122}}), during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}, and that in {{ill|lt=Shuchō|Q777413|WD=Q777413}} 1 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619}}) Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto was {{ill|lt=jointly enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}. In ancient times, it was worshipped as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} by the {{ill|lt=Isobe no Kimi clan||WD=|lt=Isobe-no-Kimi}} family, a {{ill|lt=local powerful clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066}} based in {{ill||lt=Umaji-gō|UNKNOWN}}. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} lists it as "Umajiisobe Shrine, Yasu District, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}," placing it among shikinaisha. In the medieval period, as the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|WD=Q13479654}} of {{ill||lt=Tanaka-no-shō|UNKNOWN}}, it was called "Tanaka Daimyōjin" and "Tanaka Tennō-sha." In the Sengoku period, it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Rokkaku clan|Q2975342|WD=Q2975342}}, but shrine buildings were lost in the conflicts of {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064}} and {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}. It was rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, and the current buildings date to {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), it was ranked village shrine in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in Meiji 14 it was promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precincts === ファイル:馬路石邊神社 拝殿.JPG|'''Haiden''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 神門.JPG|'''Shinmon gate''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 鳥居.JPG|'''Torii''' The current shrine buildings were constructed in {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698}}). Along the approach stands a "Mikoshi-zuka" mound said to contain a buried mikoshi. === Subsidiary shrines === [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 摂末社.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Subsidiary shrines]] The subsidiary shrines are grouped on the east side of the precincts. * Kotoshironushi Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - deities: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} * Shinmei Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaimikami}} * Hachiman Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} * Sannō Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} * Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami}} === Main festivals === * Spring festival (May 4-5){{Cite web|title="Nakasendō Moriyama-juku" complete guidebook|url=https://www.city.moriyama.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/004/384/gai.pdf |page=4|website=Moriyama and Yoshimi district revitalization committee |accessdate=2025-03-18 |language=ja}} *: On May 5, the Hōnen-odori (Moriyama City designated intangible folk cultural property) is offered. * Autumn festival (third Saturday in October) *: Sōmoku Sentō (kyōgen offering) is performed. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}{{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135}} {{ill|lt=Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|WD=Q1042787|lt=Moriyama Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=441 Umajiisobe Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うましいそへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Umajiisobe-jinja}} [[JA:馬路石邊神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11668076}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Umajiisobe Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 本殿.JPG|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|03|37.3|N|135|59|58.8|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Umajiisobe Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549}} |神事 = Hōnen-odori (May 4) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Umaji
Isobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Umajiisobe Shrine''' (うまじいそべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Its shrine crest is the "mokkō". === Enshrined deities === The principal deities are the following two: * '''{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}''' === History === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122}}), during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}, and that in {{ill|lt=Shuchō|Q777413|WD=Q777413}} 1 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619}}) Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto was {{ill|lt=jointly enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}. In ancient times, it was worshipped as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} by the {{ill|lt=Isobe no Kimi clan||WD=|lt=Isobe-no-Kimi}} family, a {{ill|lt=local powerful clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066}} based in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umaji-gō|UNKNOWN}}. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} lists it as "Umajiisobe Shrine, Yasu District, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}," placing it among shikinaisha. In the medieval period, as the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|WD=Q13479654}} of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanaka-no-shō|UNKNOWN}}, it was called "Tanaka Daimyōjin" and "Tanaka Tennō-sha." In the Sengoku period, it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Rokkaku clan|Q2975342|WD=Q2975342}}, but shrine buildings were lost in the conflicts of {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064}} and {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}. It was rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, and the current buildings date to {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), it was ranked village shrine in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in Meiji 14 it was promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precincts === ファイル:馬路石邊神社 拝殿.JPG|'''Haiden''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 神門.JPG|'''Shinmon gate''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 鳥居.JPG|'''Torii''' The current shrine buildings were constructed in {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698}}). Along the approach stands a "Mikoshi-zuka" mound said to contain a buried mikoshi. === Subsidiary shrines === [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 摂末社.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Subsidiary shrines]] The subsidiary shrines are grouped on the east side of the precincts. * Kotoshironushi Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - deities: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} * Shinmei Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaimikami}} * Hachiman Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} * Sannō Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} * Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami}} === Main festivals === * Spring festival (May 4-5){{Cite web|title="Nakasendō Moriyama-juku" complete guidebook|url=https://www.city.moriyama.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/004/384/gai.pdf |page=4|website=Moriyama and Yoshimi district revitalization committee |accessdate=2025-03-18 |language=ja}} *: On May 5, the Hōnen-odori (Moriyama City designated intangible folk cultural property) is offered. * Autumn festival (third Saturday in October) *: Sōmoku Sentō (kyōgen offering) is performed. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}{{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135}} {{ill|lt=Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|WD=Q1042787|lt=Moriyama Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=441 Umajiisobe Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うましいそへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9160005008931 == Commons category (P373) == * Umajiisobe-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県守山市吉身4丁目4-1', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Umajiisobe-jinja}} [[JA:馬路石邊神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11668076}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Umajiisobe Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 本殿.JPG|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|03|37.3|N|135|59|58.8|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Umajiisobe Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549}} |神事 = Hōnen-odori (May 4) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Umaji
Isobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Umajiisobe Shrine''' (うまじいそべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Its shrine crest is the "mokkō". === Enshrined deities === The principal deities are the following two: * '''{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}''' === History === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122}}), during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}, and that in {{ill|lt=Shuchō|Q777413|WD=Q777413}} 1 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619}}) Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto was {{ill|lt=jointly enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}. In ancient times, it was worshipped as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} by the {{ill|lt=Isobe no Kimi clan||WD=|lt=Isobe-no-Kimi}} family, a {{ill|lt=local powerful clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066}} based in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umaji-gō|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} lists it as "Umajiisobe Shrine, Yasu District, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}," placing it among shikinaisha. In the medieval period, as the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|WD=Q13479654}} of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanaka-no-shō|1=UNKNOWN}}, it was called "Tanaka Daimyōjin" and "Tanaka Tennō-sha." In the Sengoku period, it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Rokkaku clan|Q2975342|WD=Q2975342}}, but shrine buildings were lost in the conflicts of {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064}} and {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}. It was rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, and the current buildings date to {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), it was ranked village shrine in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in Meiji 14 it was promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precincts === ファイル:馬路石邊神社 拝殿.JPG|'''Haiden''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 神門.JPG|'''Shinmon gate''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 鳥居.JPG|'''Torii''' The current shrine buildings were constructed in {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698}}). Along the approach stands a "Mikoshi-zuka" mound said to contain a buried mikoshi. === Subsidiary shrines === [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 摂末社.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Subsidiary shrines]] The subsidiary shrines are grouped on the east side of the precincts. * Kotoshironushi Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - deities: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} * Shinmei Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaimikami}} * Hachiman Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} * Sannō Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} * Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami}} === Main festivals === * Spring festival (May 4-5){{Cite web|title="Nakasendō Moriyama-juku" complete guidebook|url=https://www.city.moriyama.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/004/384/gai.pdf |page=4|website=Moriyama and Yoshimi district revitalization committee |accessdate=2025-03-18 |language=ja}} *: On May 5, the Hōnen-odori (Moriyama City designated intangible folk cultural property) is offered. * Autumn festival (third Saturday in October) *: Sōmoku Sentō (kyōgen offering) is performed. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}{{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135}} {{ill|lt=Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|WD=Q1042787|lt=Moriyama Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=441 Umajiisobe Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うましいそへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Umajiisobe-jinja}} [[JA:馬路石邊神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11668076}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Umajiisobe Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 本殿.JPG|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|03|37.3|N|135|59|58.8|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Umajiisobe Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549}} |神事 = Hōnen-odori (May 4) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Umaji
Isobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Umajiisobe Shrine''' (うまじいそべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Its shrine crest is the "mokkō". === Enshrined deities === The principal deities are the following two: * '''{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}''' === History === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122}}), during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}, and that in {{ill|lt=Shuchō|Q777413|WD=Q777413}} 1 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619}}) Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto was {{ill|lt=jointly enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}. In ancient times, it was worshipped as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} by the {{ill|lt=Isobe no Kimi clan|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Isobe-no-Kimi}} family, a {{ill|lt=local powerful clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066}} based in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umaji-gō|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} lists it as "Umajiisobe Shrine, Yasu District, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}," placing it among shikinaisha. In the medieval period, as the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|WD=Q13479654}} of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanaka-no-shō|1=UNKNOWN}}, it was called "Tanaka Daimyōjin" and "Tanaka Tennō-sha." In the Sengoku period, it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Rokkaku clan|Q2975342|WD=Q2975342}}, but shrine buildings were lost in the conflicts of {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064}} and {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}. It was rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, and the current buildings date to {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), it was ranked village shrine in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in Meiji 14 it was promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precincts === ファイル:馬路石邊神社 拝殿.JPG|'''Haiden''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 神門.JPG|'''Shinmon gate''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 鳥居.JPG|'''Torii''' The current shrine buildings were constructed in {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698}}). Along the approach stands a "Mikoshi-zuka" mound said to contain a buried mikoshi. === Subsidiary shrines === [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 摂末社.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Subsidiary shrines]] The subsidiary shrines are grouped on the east side of the precincts. * Kotoshironushi Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - deities: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} * Shinmei Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaimikami}} * Hachiman Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} * Sannō Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} * Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami}} === Main festivals === * Spring festival (May 4-5){{Cite web|title="Nakasendō Moriyama-juku" complete guidebook|url=https://www.city.moriyama.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/004/384/gai.pdf |page=4|website=Moriyama and Yoshimi district revitalization committee |accessdate=2025-03-18 |language=ja}} *: On May 5, the Hōnen-odori (Moriyama City designated intangible folk cultural property) is offered. * Autumn festival (third Saturday in October) *: Sōmoku Sentō (kyōgen offering) is performed. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}{{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135}} {{ill|lt=Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|WD=Q1042787|lt=Moriyama Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=441 Umajiisobe Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うましいそへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549}} |神事 = Hōnen-odori (May 4) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Umaji
Isobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Umajiisobe Shrine''' (うまじいそべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Its shrine crest is the "mokkō". === Enshrined deities === The principal deities are the following two: * '''{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}''' === History === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122}}), during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}, and that in {{ill|lt=Shuchō|Q777413|WD=Q777413}} 1 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619}}) Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto was {{ill|lt=jointly enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}. In ancient times, it was worshipped as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} by the {{ill|lt=Isobe no Kimi clan|UNKNOWN|lt=Isobe-no-Kimi}} family, a {{ill|lt=local powerful clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066}} based in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umaji-gō|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} lists it as "Umajiisobe Shrine, Yasu District, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}," placing it among shikinaisha. In the medieval period, as the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|WD=Q13479654}} of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanaka-no-shō|1=UNKNOWN}}, it was called "Tanaka Daimyōjin" and "Tanaka Tennō-sha." In the Sengoku period, it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Rokkaku clan|Q2975342|WD=Q2975342}}, but shrine buildings were lost in the conflicts of {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064}} and {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}. It was rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, and the current buildings date to {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), it was ranked village shrine in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in Meiji 14 it was promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precincts === ファイル:馬路石邊神社 拝殿.JPG|'''Haiden''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 神門.JPG|'''Shinmon gate''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 鳥居.JPG|'''Torii''' The current shrine buildings were constructed in {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698}}). Along the approach stands a "Mikoshi-zuka" mound said to contain a buried mikoshi. === Subsidiary shrines === [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 摂末社.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Subsidiary shrines]] The subsidiary shrines are grouped on the east side of the precincts. * Kotoshironushi Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - deities: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} * Shinmei Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaimikami}} * Hachiman Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} * Sannō Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} * Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami}} === Main festivals === * Spring festival (May 4-5){{Cite web|title="Nakasendō Moriyama-juku" complete guidebook|url=https://www.city.moriyama.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/004/384/gai.pdf |page=4|website=Moriyama and Yoshimi district revitalization committee |accessdate=2025-03-18 |language=ja}} *: On May 5, the Hōnen-odori (Moriyama City designated intangible folk cultural property) is offered. * Autumn festival (third Saturday in October) *: Sōmoku Sentō (kyōgen offering) is performed. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}{{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135}} {{ill|lt=Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|WD=Q1042787|lt=Moriyama Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=441 Umajiisobe Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うましいそへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549}} |神事 = Hōnen-odori (May 4) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Umaji
Isobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Umajiisobe Shrine''' (うまじいそべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Its shrine crest is the "mokkō". === Enshrined deities === The principal deities are the following two: * '''{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}''' === History === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122}}), during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}, and that in {{ill|lt=Shuchō|Q777413|WD=Q777413}} 1 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619}}) Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto was {{ill|lt=jointly enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}. In ancient times, it was worshipped as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} by the {{ill|lt=Isobe no Kimi clan|UNKNOWN|lt=Isobe-no-Kimi}} family, a {{ill|lt=local powerful clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066}} based in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umaji-gō|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} lists it as "Umajiisobe Shrine, Yasu District, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}," placing it among shikinaisha. In the medieval period, as the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|WD=Q13479654}} of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanaka-no-shō|1=UNKNOWN}}, it was called "Tanaka Daimyōjin" and "Tanaka Tennō-sha." In the Sengoku period, it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Rokkaku clan|Q2975342|WD=Q2975342}}, but shrine buildings were lost in the conflicts of {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064}} and {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}. It was rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, and the current buildings date to {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), it was ranked village shrine in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in Meiji 14 it was promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precincts === ファイル:馬路石邊神社 拝殿.JPG|'''Haiden''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 神門.JPG|'''Shinmon gate''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 鳥居.JPG|'''Torii''' The current shrine buildings were constructed in {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698}}). Along the approach stands a "Mikoshi-zuka" mound said to contain a buried mikoshi. === Subsidiary shrines === [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 摂末社.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Subsidiary shrines]] The subsidiary shrines are grouped on the east side of the precincts. * Kotoshironushi Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - deities: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} * Shinmei Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaimikami}} * Hachiman Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} * Sannō Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} * Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami}} === Main festivals === * Spring festival (May 4-5){{Cite web|title="Nakasendō Moriyama-juku" complete guidebook|url=https://www.city.moriyama.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/004/384/gai.pdf |page=4|website=Moriyama and Yoshimi district revitalization committee |accessdate=2025-03-18 |language=ja}} *: On May 5, the Hōnen-odori (Moriyama City designated intangible folk cultural property) is offered. * Autumn festival (third Saturday in October) *: Sōmoku Sentō (kyōgen offering) is performed. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}{{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135}} {{ill|lt=Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|WD=Q1042787|lt=Moriyama Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=441 Umajiisobe Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うましいそへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Umajiisobe-jinja}} [[JA:馬路石邊神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11668076}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Umajiisobe Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 本殿.JPG|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|03|37.3|N|135|59|58.8|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Umajiisobe Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549}} |神事 = Hōnen-odori (May 4) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Umaji
Isobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Umajiisobe Shrine''' (うまじいそべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Its shrine crest is the "mokkō". === Enshrined deities === The principal deities are the following two: * '''{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}''' === History === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122}}), during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}, and that in {{ill|lt=Shuchō|Q777413|WD=Q777413}} 1 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619}}) Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto was {{ill|lt=jointly enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}. In ancient times, it was worshipped as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} by the {{ill|lt=Isobe no Kimi clan|UNKNOWN|lt=Isobe-no-Kimi}} family, a {{ill|lt=local powerful clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066}} based in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umaji-gō|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} lists it as "Umajiisobe Shrine, Yasu District, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}," placing it among shikinaisha. In the medieval period, as the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|WD=Q13479654}} of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanaka-no-shō|1=UNKNOWN}}, it was called "Tanaka Daimyōjin" and "Tanaka Tennō-sha." In the Sengoku period, it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Rokkaku clan|Q2975342|WD=Q2975342}}, but shrine buildings were lost in the conflicts of {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064}} and {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}. It was rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, and the current buildings date to {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), it was ranked village shrine in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in Meiji 14 it was promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precincts === ファイル:馬路石邊神社 拝殿.JPG|'''Haiden''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 神門.JPG|'''Shinmon gate''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 鳥居.JPG|'''Torii''' The current shrine buildings were constructed in {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698}}). Along the approach stands a "Mikoshi-zuka" mound said to contain a buried mikoshi. === Subsidiary shrines === [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 摂末社.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Subsidiary shrines]] The subsidiary shrines are grouped on the east side of the precincts. * Kotoshironushi Shrine - deity: {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - deities: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} * Shinmei Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaimikami}} * Hachiman Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} * Sannō Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} * Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami}} === Main festivals === * Spring festival (May 4-5){{Cite web|title="Nakasendō Moriyama-juku" complete guidebook|url=https://www.city.moriyama.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/004/384/gai.pdf |page=4|website=Moriyama and Yoshimi district revitalization committee |accessdate=2025-03-18 |language=ja}} *: On May 5, the Hōnen-odori (Moriyama City designated intangible folk cultural property) is offered. * Autumn festival (third Saturday in October) *: Sōmoku Sentō (kyōgen offering) is performed. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}{{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135}} {{ill|lt=Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|WD=Q1042787|lt=Moriyama Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=441 Umajiisobe Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うましいそへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Umajiisobe-jinja}} [[JA:馬路石邊神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11668076}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Umajiisobe Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 本殿.JPG|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|03|37.3|N|135|59|58.8|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Umajiisobe Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549}} |神事 = Hōnen-odori (May 4) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Umaji
Isobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Umajiisobe Shrine''' (うまじいそべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Its shrine crest is the "mokkō". === Enshrined deities === The principal deities are the following two: * '''{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}''' === History === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122}}), during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}, and that in {{ill|lt=Shuchō|Q777413|WD=Q777413}} 1 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619}}) Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto was {{ill|lt=jointly enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}. In ancient times, it was worshipped as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} by the {{ill|lt=Isobe no Kimi clan|UNKNOWN|lt=Isobe-no-Kimi}} family, a {{ill|lt=local powerful clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066}} based in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umaji-gō|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} lists it as "Umajiisobe Shrine, Yasu District, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}," placing it among shikinaisha. In the medieval period, as the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|WD=Q13479654}} of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanaka-no-shō|1=UNKNOWN}}, it was called "Tanaka Daimyōjin" and "Tanaka Tennō-sha." In the Sengoku period, it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Rokkaku clan|Q2975342|WD=Q2975342}}, but shrine buildings were lost in the conflicts of {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064}} and {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}. It was rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, and the current buildings date to {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), it was ranked village shrine in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in Meiji 14 it was promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precincts === ファイル:馬路石邊神社 拝殿.JPG|'''Haiden''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 神門.JPG|'''Shinmon gate''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 鳥居.JPG|'''Torii''' The current shrine buildings were constructed in {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698}}). Along the approach stands a "Mikoshi-zuka" mound said to contain a buried mikoshi. === Subsidiary shrines === [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 摂末社.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Subsidiary shrines]] The subsidiary shrines are grouped on the east side of the precincts. * Kotoshironushi Shrine - deity: {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - deities: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} * Shinmei Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaimikami}} * Hachiman Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} * Sannō Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} * Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami}} === Main festivals === * Spring festival (May 4-5){{Cite web|title="Nakasendō Moriyama-juku" complete guidebook|url=https://www.city.moriyama.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/004/384/gai.pdf |page=4|website=Moriyama and Yoshimi district revitalization committee |accessdate=2025-03-18 |language=ja}} *: On May 5, the Hōnen-odori (Moriyama City designated intangible folk cultural property) is offered. * Autumn festival (third Saturday in October) *: Sōmoku Sentō (kyōgen offering) is performed. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}{{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135}} {{ill|lt=Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|WD=Q1042787|lt=Moriyama Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=441 Umajiisobe Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うましいそへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Umajiisobe-jinja}} [[JA:馬路石邊神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11668076}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Umajiisobe Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 本殿.JPG|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|03|37.3|N|135|59|58.8|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Umajiisobe Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549}} |神事 = Hōnen-odori (May 4) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Umaji
Isobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Umajiisobe Shrine''' (うまじいそべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Its shrine crest is the "mokkō". === Enshrined deities === The principal deities are the following two: * '''{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}''' === History === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122}}), during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}, and that in {{ill|lt=Shuchō|Q777413|WD=Q777413}} 1 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619}}) Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto was {{ill|lt=jointly enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}. In ancient times, it was worshipped as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} by the {{ill|lt=Isobe no Kimi clan|UNKNOWN|lt=Isobe-no-Kimi}} family, a {{ill|lt=local powerful clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066}} based in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umaji-gō|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} lists it as "Umajiisobe Shrine, Yasu District, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}," placing it among shikinaisha. In the medieval period, as the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|WD=Q13479654}} of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanaka-no-shō|1=UNKNOWN}}, it was called "Tanaka Daimyōjin" and "Tanaka Tennō-sha." In the Sengoku period, it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Rokkaku clan|Q2975342|WD=Q2975342}}, but shrine buildings were lost in the conflicts of {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064}} and {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}. It was rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, and the current buildings date to {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), it was ranked village shrine in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in Meiji 14 it was promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precincts === ファイル:馬路石邊神社 拝殿.JPG|'''Haiden''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 神門.JPG|'''Shinmon gate''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 鳥居.JPG|'''Torii''' The current shrine buildings were constructed in {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698}}). Along the approach stands a "Mikoshi-zuka" mound said to contain a buried mikoshi. === Subsidiary shrines === [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 摂末社.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Subsidiary shrines]] The subsidiary shrines are grouped on the east side of the precincts. * Kotoshironushi Shrine - deity: {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - deities: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} * Shinmei Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaimikami}} * Hachiman Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} * Sannō Shrine - deity: {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} * Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami}} === Main festivals === * Spring festival (May 4-5){{Cite web|title="Nakasendō Moriyama-juku" complete guidebook|url=https://www.city.moriyama.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/004/384/gai.pdf |page=4|website=Moriyama and Yoshimi district revitalization committee |accessdate=2025-03-18 |language=ja}} *: On May 5, the Hōnen-odori (Moriyama City designated intangible folk cultural property) is offered. * Autumn festival (third Saturday in October) *: Sōmoku Sentō (kyōgen offering) is performed. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}{{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135}} {{ill|lt=Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|WD=Q1042787|lt=Moriyama Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=441 Umajiisobe Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うましいそへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Umajiisobe-jinja}} [[JA:馬路石邊神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11668076}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Umajiisobe Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 本殿.JPG|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|03|37.3|N|135|59|58.8|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Umajiisobe Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549}} |神事 = Hōnen-odori (May 4) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Umaji
Isobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Umajiisobe Shrine''' (うまじいそべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Its shrine crest is the "mokkō". === Enshrined deities === The principal deities are the following two: * '''{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}''' === History === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122}}), during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}, and that in {{ill|lt=Shuchō|Q777413|WD=Q777413}} 1 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619}}) Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto was {{ill|lt=jointly enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}. In ancient times, it was worshipped as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} by the {{ill|lt=Isobe no Kimi clan|UNKNOWN|lt=Isobe-no-Kimi}} family, a {{ill|lt=local powerful clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066}} based in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umaji-gō|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} lists it as "Umajiisobe Shrine, Yasu District, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}," placing it among shikinaisha. In the medieval period, as the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|WD=Q13479654}} of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanaka-no-shō|1=UNKNOWN}}, it was called "Tanaka Daimyōjin" and "Tanaka Tennō-sha." In the Sengoku period, it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Rokkaku clan|Q2975342|WD=Q2975342}}, but shrine buildings were lost in the conflicts of {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064}} and {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}. It was rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, and the current buildings date to {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), it was ranked village shrine in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in Meiji 14 it was promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Precincts === ファイル:馬路石邊神社 拝殿.JPG|'''Haiden''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 神門.JPG|'''Shinmon gate''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 鳥居.JPG|'''Torii''' The current shrine buildings were constructed in {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698}}). Along the approach stands a "Mikoshi-zuka" mound said to contain a buried mikoshi. === Subsidiary shrines === [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 摂末社.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Subsidiary shrines]] The subsidiary shrines are grouped on the east side of the precincts. * Kotoshironushi Shrine - deity: {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - deities: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} * Shinmei Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaimikami}} * Hachiman Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} * Sannō Shrine - deity: {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto}} * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} * Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami}} === Main festivals === * Spring festival (May 4-5){{Cite web|title="Nakasendō Moriyama-juku" complete guidebook|url=https://www.city.moriyama.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/004/384/gai.pdf |page=4|website=Moriyama and Yoshimi district revitalization committee |accessdate=2025-03-18 |language=ja}} *: On May 5, the Hōnen-odori (Moriyama City designated intangible folk cultural property) is offered. * Autumn festival (third Saturday in October) *: Sōmoku Sentō (kyōgen offering) is performed. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}{{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135}} {{ill|lt=Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|WD=Q1042787|lt=Moriyama Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=441 Umajiisobe Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うましいそへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9160005008931 == Commons category (P373) == * Umajiisobe-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県守山市吉身4丁目4-1', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Umajiisobe-jinja}} [[JA:馬路石邊神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11668076}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Umajiisobe Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 本殿.JPG|270px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|03|37.3|N|135|59|58.8|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Umajiisobe Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122|1=663}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}{{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|WD=Q2549|1=May 4}} |神事 = Hōnen-odori (May 4) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Umaji
Isobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Umajiisobe Shrine''' (うまじいそべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. Its shrine crest is the "mokkō". === Enshrined deities === The principal deities are the following two: * '''{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}''' === History === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|WD=Q31122|1=663}}), during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, and that in {{ill|lt=Shuchō|Q777413|WD=Q777413|1=Shuchō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619|1=686}}) Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto was {{ill|lt=jointly enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}. In ancient times, it was worshipped as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} by the {{ill|lt=Isobe no Kimi clan|UNKNOWN|lt=Isobe-no-Kimi}} family, a {{ill|lt=local powerful clan|Q84066|WD=Q84066|1=Gōzoku}} based in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umaji-gō|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} lists it as "Umajiisobe Shrine, Yasu District, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}," placing it among shikinaisha. In the medieval period, as the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|WD=Q13479654|1=Chinjusha}} of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanaka-no-shō|1=UNKNOWN}}, it was called "Tanaka Daimyōjin" and "Tanaka Tennō-sha." In the Sengoku period, it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Rokkaku clan|Q2975342|WD=Q2975342|1=Rokkaku clan}}, but shrine buildings were lost in the conflicts of {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064|1=Genki (era)}} and {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}}. It was rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, and the current buildings date to {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266|1=Bunkyū}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698|1=1862}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}), it was ranked village shrine in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and in Meiji 14 it was promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Precincts === ファイル:馬路石邊神社 拝殿.JPG|'''Haiden''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 神門.JPG|'''Shinmon gate''' ファイル:馬路石邊神社 鳥居.JPG|'''Torii''' The current shrine buildings were constructed in {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266|1=Bunkyū}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698|1=1862}}). Along the approach stands a "Mikoshi-zuka" mound said to contain a buried mikoshi. === Subsidiary shrines === [[ファイル:馬路石邊神社 摂末社.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Subsidiary shrines]] The subsidiary shrines are grouped on the east side of the precincts. * Kotoshironushi Shrine - deity: {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - deities: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto / Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} * Shinmei Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaimikami|1=Amaterasu}} * Hachiman Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * Sannō Shrine - deity: {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} * Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} * Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} === Main festivals === * Spring festival (May 4-5){{Cite web|title="Nakasendō Moriyama-juku" complete guidebook|url=https://www.city.moriyama.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/004/384/gai.pdf |page=4|website=Moriyama and Yoshimi district revitalization committee |accessdate=2025-03-18 |language=ja}} *: On May 5, the Hōnen-odori (Moriyama City designated intangible folk cultural property) is offered. * Autumn festival (third Saturday in October) *: Sōmoku Sentō (kyōgen offering) is performed. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}Yoshimi 4-4-1 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}}{{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135|1=Biwako Line}} {{ill|lt=Moriyama Station (Shiga)|Q1042787|WD=Q1042787|lt=Moriyama Station|1=Moriyama Station (Shiga)}} (about 15 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=441 Umajiisobe Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うましいそへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Umajiisobe Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]
